steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks to emphasize when she collected both samples. That means the answer must name both specimens and give the collection date for each.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Thousands of botanical samples collected by Ynés Mexía can now be found online': doesn't mention either specific specimen or their collection dates. ✗
  • Choice B — 'both specimens are members of the same family, found in different locations': emphasizes geography (Minas Gerais vs. Chihuahua), not timing. ✗
  • Choice C — 'On June 26 in 1930, Mexía collected Trixis antimenorrhoea': gives a date for only one specimen; Stephanomeria thurberi is not mentioned. ✗
  • Choice D — 'collected Trixis antimenorrhoea in June of 1930, after collecting Stephanomeria thurberi in May of 1929': names both specimens and gives the collection date for each, with 'after' making the chronological sequence explicit. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
